{{Infobox book
| name = Of Sea and Shadow
| slug = ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow
| precedes = [[Of Shadow and Sea (novel)|Of Shadow and Sea]]
| follows = [[City of Light (novel)|City of Light]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow.jpg|250px]]
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The story of *Of Sea and [[Shadow]]* follows Calder Marten{{!}}Captain Calder [[Marten]], a skilled 'Reader' capable of sensing the [[Intent]] of objects, as he navigates a world of imperial politics, ancient magic, and personal betrayal. The narrative is woven through non-linear timelines, revealing that [[Calder]]'s life was irrevocably changed eleven to thirteen years prior when a failed attempt to steal an [[Imperial]] quill led to his father [[Rojric]]'s arrest and [[Calder]]'s eventual forced service as an [[Imperial Navigator]] after a high-stakes prison break. During that past rebellion, [[Calder]] struck a dangerous bargain with the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]], trading an [[Emperor's key]] for a [[Lyathatan]] to rescue his father, only to witness [[Rojric]]'s execution by [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]].

In the present, [[Calder]] commands the magically unique ship *[[The Testament]]*. His life is upended when his wife, [[Jerri]], is attacked by the assassin [[Shera]] and disappears, leading [[Calder]] to believe she has been taken or lost. This personal grief intersects with a massive political conspiracy when [[Calder]] is hired by the [[Guild]] Chronicler [[Naberius Clayborn]] to find treasure to fund a new Emperor. The mission leads the crew to a fleshy, disturbing island where they discover the [[Blackwatch Guild]]—led by [[Calder]]'s mother, [[Alsa Grayweather]]—is already searching for the [[Heart of Nakothi]] to bind it to a new monarch.

As the crew navigates dangerous waters like the [[Silver Spires]], they are caught in a multi-sided conflict between the [[Blackwatch]], the Consultants' Guild{{!}}Consultant Guild, and a mysterious cult known as the [[Sleepless]]. During a chaotic battle at the Heart's location, [[Calder]] learns the devastating truth: [[Jerri]] is not merely missing, but is a member of the [[Sleepless]]. Amidst the supernatural carnage involving the monstrous [[Children of Nakothi]], [[Naberius]] begins a maddening transformation by bonding with the Heart. To prevent total catastrophe and escape the island's escalating chaos, [[Calder]] is forced to throw the Heart into the sea.

The conflict reaches a fever pitch at [[Gray Island]] and the [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]]. [[Calder]] attempts to navigate the complex legalities of his [[Blackwatch]] Authority{{!}}authority to rescue his father, only to discover that [[Rojric]] and the prison staff are being manipulated by an unnatural force. As [[Naberius]] escapes his bonds to pursue the Heart's power, [[Calder]]'s crew finds themselves fighting off [[Imperial Guards]] and [[Consultants]] alike. In a final, violent confrontation at a ruined arena, the crew suffers heavy losses, including the death of [[Urzaia Woodsman]] at the hands of the [[Consultants]]. Amidst the arrival of a colossal Handmaidens of Nakothi{{!}}Handmaiden of Nakothi, [[Calder]] manages to capture the [[Consultant]] [[Meia]] and execute the now-mad [[Naberius]]. The story concludes with [[Calder]] meeting with high-ranking officials, including [[General Teach]] and [[Bliss]], where he uses an [[Imperial]] crown to propose himself as a mortal candidate for the throne, even as an ominous presence threatens to emerge from the shadows.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Calder Marten{{!}}Captain Calder [[Marten]] and his crew hunt a massive, lightning-based creature called a [[Kameira]] during a violent storm using alchemical thunderlights and the ship's unique abilities. After an unsuccessful cannon shot causes the creature to attack, [[Calder]] summons a bound sea monster known as a [[Lyathatan]] to seize and kill the prey. Following the battle, [[Calder]] discovers a mysterious, magically scorched message in his ship's hold offering a high-stakes mission involving The Empire{{!}}the Empire and a massive financial reward.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] and his father, [[Rojric]], pose as the "noble family of [[Fairstreet]]" to purchase an ancient [[Imperial]] quill from Karls Dunwood{{!}}Mister Dunwood, using [[Calder]]'s ability to "[[Read]]" the [[Intent]] of objects to confirm its authenticity. Later that night, they return in disguise to steal the artifact but are ambushed by the [[Imperial Guard]], who have been waiting for them. While [[Rojric]] is arrested, [[Calder]] uses his [[Intent]] to hide a stolen copper key in his clothing and is told he will be sent to live with his mother.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|After repairing his ship, *[[The Testament]]*, Calder Marten{{!}}Captain Calder [[Marten]] meets his new clients: [[Naberius Clayborn]], a [[Guild]] Chronicler, and his silent bodyguard, [[Tristania]]. [[Naberius]] reveals he is seeking treasure to fund the installation of a new Emperor and offers a massive payment of ten thousand [[goldmarks]] for the voyage. Despite [[Calder]]'s skepticism regarding [[Naberius]]'s fears of the [[Consultant's Guild]], the crew agrees to the mission and prepares to depart at dawn.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Thirteen years ago, [[Calder]] is brought to meet his mother, [[Alsa Grayweather]], a member of the [[Blackwatch Guild]] who possesses the power of a Reader. After [[Calder]] attempts to bluff his way through a conversation about his future, [[Alsa]] demonstrates her superior abilities by [[investing]] a pillow with enough [[Intent]] to stop a bullet. The chapter concludes with [[Alsa]] deciding to personally oversee [[Calder]]'s training in various combat skills.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|During a quiet night in the bay, [[Calder]] is shocked to discover his wife, [[Jerri]], using magical green flames to fight off an assassin named [[Shera]]. After a chaotic skirmish on deck, the assassin pushes [[Jerri]] overboard into the sea and escapes. The following morning, a devastated [[Calder]] learns that [[Jerri]] is missing and decides to steer the ship toward [[Gray Island]], the headquarters of the [[Consultants]], in a desperate attempt to find her.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Thirteen-year-old [[Calder]] uses the [[Emperor's key]] to experience visions of the monarch's cruelty, leading him to believe [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] is a monster. While hiding the dangerous key, [[Calder]] meets his new tutors—the [[Witness]] brothers [[Artur]] and [[Vorus]]—and a fellow student named [[Jyrine]]. The group begins their studies in philosophy and history, though [[Calder]] and [[Jyrine]] remain eager to learn more about the secrets of the Witnesses and the [[Blackwatch]].}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|While navigating the dangerous [[Silver Spires]], Calder Marten{{!}}Captain Calder struggles with grief and a sense of recklessness following his wife [[Jerri]]'s betrayal. After being drugged into a forced smile by [[Petal]] and receiving a stern warning from [[Andel]] about his dangerous course, the crew is suddenly interrupted by a 'wormcloud' storm. The chapter concludes with the crew working together to fight off falling, venomous worms while [[Naberius]] warns of even greater monstrosities awaiting them at their destination.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Fourteen-year-old [[Calder]] and his friend [[Jyrine]] sneak out to investigate his mother's secret work, discovering a mysterious ship called [[The Testament]] being constructed at the harbor. After a brief duel with a [[Navigator captain]], [[Calder]] is confronted by [[Bliss]], the [[Guild]] Blackwatch Guild Head{{!}}Head of the Blackwatch, who subjects him to a terrifying supernatural vision. Despite his fear, [[Calder]] accepts a [[Blackwatch badge]] and officially joins the [[Guild]] to help with their mysterious project.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] and his crew arrive at a fleshy, disturbing island to assist [[Naberius]], only to discover that the [[Blackwatch Guild]] is already present and has orchestrated the mission. [[Calder]] reunites with his mother, [[Alsa Grayweather]], who reveals that they are searching for the [[Heart of Nakothi]] to bind it to a new Emperor. Meanwhile, the imprisoned [[Jerri]] communicates with a mysterious voice from a void, learning that the [[Sleepless]] cabal is also moving to secure the Heart.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Twelve]] years ago, [[Calder Marten]] integrates into the [[Blackwatch]] guild, receiving seven specialized Iron{{!}}iron nails used to paralyze [[Elders]]. While working on the ship *[[The Testament]]*, [[Calder]] witnesses multiple prisoners committing suicide by jumping from the [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]]. Motivated by these events and his desire to see his own imprisoned father, [[Calder]] agrees to partner with [[Jyrine]] to plan a prison break.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|While attempting to harvest a [[Great Elder]]'s heart from a fleshy crater, [[Calder]] and his crew are ambushed by the [[Consultants]] and subsequently attacked by the monstrous [[Children of Nakothi]]. As the battle erupts, a mysterious blond-haired woman with clawed hands intervenes to fight the creatures and descends into the pit. [[Calder]], fighting for survival against grotesque monsters, realizes the true target of the assault is the Heart itself.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|During a chaotic battle against the [[Children of Nakothi]], [[Calder]] retrieves the [[Heart of Nakothi]] from [[Naberius]] and survives a duel with the [[Consultant]] [[Shera]], who reveals that [[Calder]]'s wife is dead. To facilitate an escape, [[Naberius]] performs a ritual by sacrificing [[Tristania]] to deceive the monsters, allowing the group to retreat to their ship. As they prepare to depart, the arrival of [[Cheska Bennett]]'s ship provides hope, and [[Urzaia]] vows to use his abilities to track down the woman responsible for [[Jerri]]'s death.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] and [[Jerri]] spend months researching how to break Rojric{{!}}Rojric Marten out of [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]], learning about the legal limits of [[Calder]]'s [[Blackwatch]] Authority{{!}}authority and the existence of ancient technology designed to detect [[Readers]]. After discovering that prisons use devices to sense [[Intent]], [[Jyrine]] proposes using her non-Reader status to infiltrate the facility. The chapter concludes when [[Calder]] receives an official letter from the prison warden inviting him to visit his father.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] meets with [[Cheska Bennett]] and several [[Guild Heads]] to discuss the theft of the [[Heart of Nakothi]] by the [[Consultants]]. During the meeting, [[Naberius Clayborn]] reveals he has already begun bonding with the Heart and is being positioned as a candidate for the new Emperor. The council decides that [[Calder]] will transport [[Naberius]] toward the [[Gray Island]] to await further orders.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder Marten]] visits his father, [[Rojric]], in the [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]] and is initially unsettled by his father's uncharacteristically pleasant and compliant demeanor. Using his abilities as a Reader, [[Calder]] touches objects in the cell to sense [[Rojric]]'s true emotions, discovering that his father is actually in immense pain and desperate to escape. After confirming [[Rojric]]'s desire to flee, [[Calder]] realizes that both his father and the prison staff are being controlled by an unnatural force that compels them to maintain a facade of happiness.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|During a high-stakes council meeting, [[Alsa]] overhears [[Naberius]] plotting to execute [[Calder]] and his crew to seize control of their ship. [[Alsa]] attempts to hold the [[Magister]] hostage to buy time for her son, while [[Bliss]] secretly stows away on [[Calder]]'s vessel. Once aboard, [[Bliss]] reveals to a shocked [[Calder]] that [[Naberius]] intends to kill him and provides information regarding a potential void transmission from the [[Gray Island]].}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|While [[Imperial Guards]] attempt to detain the crew of [[The Testament]], [[Urzaia Woodsman]] uses his [[Sandborn Hydra]] power to break free from his chains and fight his way to the deck. Amidst a chaotic battle involving musket fire and ship-based combat, Calder Marten{{!}}Captain Calder uses his connection to the vessel to tilt the deck and repel the invaders. To secure their escape, [[Calder]] decides to launch a risky boarding maneuver to take [[Naberius Clayborn]] hostage from the nearby ship, [[The Eternal]].}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|After a tense standoff on [[The Eternal]], [[Calder]] and [[Urzaia]] escape with the captive [[Naberius]] as [[Bliss]] engages [[General Teach]] in a display of power. Meanwhile, [[Jerri]] receives a mysterious black metal collar through a void transmission while imprisoned on the [[Gray Island]]. Upon approaching the island, [[Calder]] and his crew decide to attempt to secure the [[Heart of Nakothi]] as leverage for their freedom, prompting [[Calder]] to retrieve a mysterious lockbox from his cabin.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Eleven]] years ago, [[Calder]] and [[Jyrine]] devise a clandestine plan to break [[Calder]]'s father, Rojric{{!}}Rojric Marten, out of prison using [[Jyrine]]'s disguises and [[Calder]]'s magical [[Emperor's key]]. While preparing for the operation, [[Calder]] interacts with his mother, [[Alsa]], who introduces him to a strange [[Elderspawn]] creature named [[Shuffles]]. As the mission begins, [[Calder]] is overcome with anxiety regarding the potential consequences for his mother and his own career if the plan fails.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Calder Marten{{!}}Captain Calder [[Marten]] and his crew arrive at the [[Gray Island]] to negotiate with [[Kerian]], a [[Consultant]], regarding the location of the [[Heart of Nakothi]] and [[Calder]]'s missing wife. During the meeting, [[Kerian]] reveals that [[Calder]]'s wife is a member of a cult called the [[Sleepless]] and offers him a supervised visit in exchange for [[Naberius]] and the Heart. To gain leverage, [[Calder]] uses an [[Imperial]] crown to magically compel the [[Consultants]] to obey his commands, allowing him to take control of the meeting and head toward the prisoners.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Jerri]] infiltrates the [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]] using forged documents and a monster named [[Shuffles]] to distract guards, eventually using her [[Soulbound powers]] to burn a hole through Rojric{{!}}Rojric Marten's cell window. [[Calder]] arrives at the prison rocks after his raft sinks, and he reunites with [[Jerri]] and his father to plan an escape. Facing a standoff with armed guards, [[Calder]] prepares a desperate plan involving his [[Blackwatch coat]] and his own blood.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Naberius]] escapes his bonds and follows the song of the [[Heart of the Dead Mother]], driven by a desire to reshape the world. Meanwhile, in the prison cells, [[Jerri]] survives a tense confrontation with [[Shera]] and later faces a devastating interrogation by [[Calder Marten]], who discovers her deep ties to the [[Sleepless]]. As [[Calder]] leaves [[Jerri]] behind in anger, she prepares to use an Elder artifacts{{!}}Elder artifact to attempt a desperate escape.}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Naberius]] discovers and claims the [[Heart of Nakothi]], undergoing a maddening transformation as he attempts to bond with its power. Meanwhile, [[Calder]] and his crew witness a chaotic series of events, including [[Lucan]] awakening a deadly blade and the arrival of a colossal Handmaidens of Nakothi{{!}}Handmaiden of Nakothi. To escape the island's escalating supernatural chaos, [[Calder]] ultimately throws the Heart into the sea.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Eleven]] years ago, a desperate [[Calder Marten]] summons the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]] to save him and his companions from drowning, striking a bargain to trade [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]]'s copper key for assistance. [[Kelarac]] fulfills his end by sending a [[Lyathatan]], which [[Calder]] then uses to awaken the true power of the ship, [[The Testament]], allowing them to escape and rescue his father from prison. After recovering from a Reader’s burn{{!}}Reader's burn, [[Calder]] is startled by the arrival of an unknown presence that fills him with instinctive fear.}}
* '''Chapter 25:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] meets with the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]] in a frozen vision, receiving a branded mark and a powerful saber in exchange for the [[Heart of the Dead Mother]]. After escaping an [[Elderspawn]], [[Calder]] arrives at the ruined arena to find [[Urzaia]] fighting a [[Consultant]] and [[Shera]], only to witness the [[Consultants]] use an Awakened blade to kill [[Urzaia]]. As [[Calder]] prepares to fight the remaining three [[Consultants]], the Handmaidens of Nakothi{{!}}Handmaiden of Nakothi arrives and tears off the arena's roof.}}
* '''Chapter 26:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Eleven]] years ago, [[Calder Marten]] is captured by [[General Jarelys Teach]] and brought before [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] to face trial for his role in a prison break. During the audience, [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] reveals that [[Calder]] has unintentionally become a [[Soulbound]] by awakening the ship *[[The Testament]]*, and he sentences [[Calder]]'s father, [[Rojric]], to death. After witnessing his father's execution, a devastated [[Calder]] is taken into [[Imperial]] custody to serve The Empire{{!}}the Empire as a [[Navigator]].}}
* '''Chapter 27:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|After the [[Handmaiden]] tears open the arena roof, [[Calder]] witnesses the death of [[Urzaia]] and coordinates a cannon strike from his ship, [[The Testament]], to attack the creature. Meanwhile, [[Jerri]] is rescued from her cell by a mysterious cabal via a void portal, allowing her to reunite with her [[Vessel]]. [[Calder]] arrives at the prison too late to find [[Jerri]] but manages to incapacitate and capture the [[Consultant]] [[Meia]] as he retreats toward his ship.}}
* '''Chapter 28:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Eleven]] years prior to the present, [[Bliss]] takes [[Alsa Grayweather]] into her personal custody after learning that [[Alsa]]'s son, [[Calder]], caused a prison break and stole a ship. In the present day, [[Calder]]'s crew executes their prisoner [[Naberius]] to prevent further madness before meeting with [[General Teach]], [[Cheska Bennett]], and [[Bliss]]. During this meeting, [[Calder]] presents [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]]'s crown and proposes himself as a mortal candidate for the throne, though the chapter ends with an ominous warning that someone has escaped.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Jerri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Naberius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Andel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Lyathatan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Urzaia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elderspawn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder Marten]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Foster]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Reader (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Emperor (Elder Empire)|Emperor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Tristania]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Jyrine]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Shuffles]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Shera]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Bliss]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Petal]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Alsa Grayweather]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Jarelys Teach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Chronicler (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Alsa]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Lucan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Andel Petronus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Cheska Bennett]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Rojric]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Naberius Clayborn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Handmaiden]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Dalton Foster]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Mekendi Maxeus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Urzaia Woodsman]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Jyrine Tessella Marten]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Artur]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Dead Mother]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Cheska]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Silent One]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Karls Dunwood]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Nathanael Bareius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Vorus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Child of Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[General Teach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Artur Belfry]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kthanikahr]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Guild Head]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kerian]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Blackwatch]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Consultants]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Gray Island]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Aion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Imperial Guard]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sleepless]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Witnesses (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Aion Sea]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Candle Bay]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Consultant]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Empire (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Estyr Six]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Capital (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Imperial Guards]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Regents]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Great Elders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Imperial Palace (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Navigators (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Alchemist's Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Erin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Izyrians]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Consultant's Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Champions]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Aurelian Empire]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Reading]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Awaken]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Heart of Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Heart (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Eternal]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Emperor's crown (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Soulbound Vessel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[quicklamp]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Intent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Soulbound]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kameira]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Children of Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Vessel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Othaghor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Great Elder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Champion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Heartlander]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Bellowing Horror]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Awakening]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Stormwing (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Watchman (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Children of the Dead Mother]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Elder (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}}

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
